creator: mad-scientist@safe-mail.net
text:
Thanks for the response.

It's been a while since I've been on a BSD, so I'm a bit rusty.  First
let me clarify that I'm setting the DISPLAY as:

setenv DISPLAY :0 (csh) OR
export DISPLAY=:0 (bash/sh)
If those are wrong, let me know.

I tried the Xorg --configure && Xorg -config /path/to/xorg.new thing.
While the X log no longer tells me it can't load the radeon driver
(seems to be using the ati driver), it still doesn't startup.  In most
cases, I just get a grey background, with no mouse or spinning cursor.

Running from a user account, I get the following:

Xorg or the xinit script:  grey screen, no mouse.  The screen appears to
'dematerialize' over time.  startx gnome: crashes on 'illegal command
xterm'.  Also complains at startup/shutdown about 'Bad display name :0'.

Running gdm from the root acct, I just get the grey bkgd, no mouse.

So at this point, I'm really not at all sure if this is X related (I
think so), or how much has to do with gnome.

Here are the files you requested.  By boot-verbose, I have assumed you
meant boot in verbose mode, and send a copy of /var/log/messages.  But
before I go off into the files, thanks guys.
